▲ Page top
Found after 15 hours
QuoteReply
# Aug 27 2002 at 2:22 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591751241211
View User Forum
10 posts
Took me 15 hours to get him to spawn, but here's a bit more info if you need it.

To get to where Quid spawns, I'll assume you're coming out of the Feerrott zone. At the first junction, go right. Run into the open area to the left. You'll see a hill straight ahead or slightly to your right along with some ruins. Glaron the Wicked spawns in those ruins. If you turn left more and run back through the little hills and valleys, you'll come across a gypsy camp.

Kill all roaming bugs and critters from the gypsy camp to where Glaron the Wicked stands. This may be overkill, but it's not like you're going to be doing anything anyways.

You can kill Glaron the Wicked (no negative faction hit), but he's for the shaman epic and has a spawn time of 3 hours, so ask around before you and kill him; someone may need him. See note at the end about Glaron.

If you're heading from Glaron to the Gypsy camp, Quid will spawn to the left of the camp near the back wall. He'll begin to walk in an almost direct line towards Glaron the Wicked, so just travel this area often while you're on you killing spree.

You'll know Quid when you see him. He's an erudite pally and he's the only human type npc that roams this side of the hill. If you're an evil class/race, he'll be KOS to you.

If you're a fear kiting class, he'll lay hands when the first fear breaks, so be prepared for his hp to jump all the way back up.

I killed him as a 38 necro with little trouble. He may be 35th level, but he fights like he's about 30th. In my case, I did this to kill him: Darkness, send pet, fear, Venom of the Snake, Boiling Blood, lifetap dot. When fear broke, VOS finished, so I cast fear again, then VOS one more time. I used about 40% of my mana in all and spent much of the fight on my butt.

He's not overly difficult but the LOH does surprise you if you're not expecting it.

About Glaron, he's a little more difficult than Quid, but only because he opens with a Harm Touch. I've actually seen him HT twice in one fight, which, needless to say, caught me off guard. Same tactic that worked on Quid will work on Glaron, but don't send the pet until after he's feared. Otherwise, your pet will eat the HT.

Vahlen Winds <Sword of Fate>
Prexus
▲ Page top

# Jul 23 2002 at 7:38 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592255269632
8 posts
I guess I'm quite the lucky chanty. I was meandering (sp? it's late/early here )through the zone and was invited into a group to crack up the melees and the cleric. Well lo and behold Quid pops and we drop him. I won the roll.

Now I have one for sale on Vaz. Shoot a tell to Scheherazade if you're interested.

Quid Still Spawns
QuoteReply
# Sep 07 2001 at 12:44 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591761181041
1 post
I've been hunting HG's in Rathe Mountains for about 3 weeks, and I check my tracking every time I zone in and out through Feerrott. I've seen him on track 2 times, killed him both times, and he dropped the Hood both times. One time at night, one time during day.

Good luck to all.

Ranger of the 38th season
Ayonae Ro
▲ Page top
info on this bastard
QuoteReply
# Aug 22 2001 at 6:06 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592757687988
View User Forum
111 posts
hi all, in my endless search to better equip my baby enchanter i learn about quid rilstone and his pretty hat. i decide to go over to rathe mountains and take it from him.

well i followed all the advice and prepaired myself for an easy half day, day camp. i am now about 2 days into my camp, have seen quid TWICE, both times with that god awful hammer. first of all, quid is RARE. many of these posts give the impression that you kill greens for an hour or two, then he pops. not true. takes at least 5 hours of killing greens, if even that. second, the hood is not as common as they are leading you to believe. like i said, 2 straight kills and no hood.

i am a sicko and will continue to kill my mind by slaughtering level 3 beatles till i get this hood. now its more about principle though.

good luck to all of you.

edit:
i have now camped him for 4 days and still havent seen him after his last appearance, which was a few hours after the patch. he is very very rare. i hate to say it but even pyzjn is easier than this camp so be prepaired.

further edit:
got the hood today, was there clearing trash for only a half hour when he spawned half way inbetween the pally camp and the gypsy camp. in case anyone was wondering, he can spawn at both night and day, as he spawned for me this time at 1pm game time.
▲ Page top
Got this piece
QuoteReply
# Aug 21 2001 at 4:32 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592249894450
12 posts

I just transfered to the tunare server from karana about 3 weeks ago. Since I had to give up all my stuff on the old server to move to the new one , i had to go in search of items to replace my naked self =)
I my search for things i came across this nice little item , so i decided when i got transfered i would go give it a look and see if i could get lucky. Well a mid lvl monk friend went with me to keep me from being bored by myself haha. Well i thought of the gypsies on the Lake Rathe Zone side of the mountain zone. so we began killing all the bears in the area cause that is all that is there cept for an orc camp. I had a funny feelin i was in the wrong place haha. Which turns out i was. I started asking for the /loc of the paly camp ...quiet returns at first, but then a nice lil dark elf lady said she would take me there =) so i buffed her up real nice and thanked her for her kindness.
For a easy way i can think to tell you to get to this camp is , after you zone in from Ferrott and you get to the very first split in the valley that goes left and right. Just follow to the right side. It will come out into a big opening with some ruins and some wooden spike sticking outward from the ground. To the left of those spikes you will see the ruins of a building , if you head straight into that direction that should put you pretty close to the hill that the paly camp is on(if i remember correctly) on the top of that hill you will find at one end a orc camp with 3 orcs in it and a totem pole kinda deal in there with it. At the other end of the hill is the paly/gypsie camp. to the right of the camp down at the bottom of the hill is another inn.

Now what i did, pretty simple was made a pet. everything is super green... fire beetles, skelys , once in a while a grizzly bear. i got my first tash spell and began casting it on everything , since it used like 10 mana i didnt use anything hardly haha, i would have 5-10 monsters on me haha , would just let my pet with haste on go wild on him. I even kept the orcs at the other end of the hill cleared out , i never left the top of the hill , stuff just keeps comin across there, just keep killing it all.
I am guessing that i got lucky on it , since it took about 15-20 minutes tops of me doing this to get Quid to pop. then i put haste on my pet, beserker str, and let him go to town. now i was lvl 51 when i did this , so this was not a hard fight at all haha, but even in the 40s this should not be hard at all either, just haste your pet, use beserker series on your pet and you will solo this guy like cake. nothin to it, i could have prolly killed him in a couple nukes, but was no matter.
low and behold he dropped the hood!! Woot!
very nice chanter item indeed. I had runed cowl on other server with the INT on it , but the CHA ratio compared to the INT leaves this item to be better, and you can substitute other items for more int in other areas to balance it out, plus it has a lil resist to it , and who doesnt need a lil extra resist? =)

Well anyways, i hope this helps you out findin the camp and getting the item! Goodluck to yous and hope you are as lucky as i was and get it fast ! =) *wave*

QuoteReply
# Aug 07 2001 at 7:49 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593361483967

Last night for 4 hours, I hunted the bears, skeletons, orcs, and all the random creatures looking for Quid Rilstone. Unfortunately, I could not get him to spawn. Instead, a character called "Glaron the wicked" spawned in the ruins (with the catapult). Is there any relation, has anyone seen "Quid Rilstone" recently? I just want to know, so It doesnt seem like I am wasting my time.

This hood rocks. It gives the elemental illusion a longer tail and has alot of cha. I camped this with my lvl 35 enc and it spawned in about an hour after i killed everything between the Gypsies and Paladin camp. This guy also heals his self everytime he gets around 25% health and killed me once.

just killd him this weekend. I logged on , ported to Feerrott then ran to Rathe Mtns...He was on track so I took care of business. He conned even at 34, and dropped the Siryn Hair Hood. Hits for about 70-80, and did a loh..
